title: dracophyllum rosmarinifolium
intro: Dracophyllum rosmarinifolium, also known as the needle bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of eastern Australia and is characterized by its small, needle-like leaves and white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system function. The needle bush can be prepared for medicinal use in a variety of ways, including as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
